message:
fix(core): initialise checksum tail in new directory blocks

When ext4_add_dir_entry allocates a fresh directory block on a
filesystem with metadata checksums (reserved_tail=12), the trailing
12 bytes were left as zeros.  parse_dir_block interprets rec_len=0 as
block_size (via rec_len_from_disk), producing a phantom entry whose
rec_len overflows past the block boundary.  This caused a silent parse
error that made lookup_in_dir_block and read_dir return no entries for
the newly allocated block—the root cause of fuse_spec_i5 failing at
file 169 (the first entry in the second directory block).

Fix: write the ext4 dir-entry checksum tail sentinel (inode=0,
rec_len=12, name_len=0, file_type=0xDE) into the reserved tail area
of every newly allocated directory block.

Also:
- Add open_writable_ext4_mkfs() helper that creates a real ext4 image
  via mkfs.ext4 for more realistic testing.
- Add create_many_files_across_dir_block_boundary_mkfs regression test
  (250 files with create + lookup + readdir + unlink verification).
- Add fuse_create_and_remove_across_dir_block_boundary E2E test.
- Remove leftover tracing::error! in read_dir_with_scope.

bd-p3sd

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
